notes.title: Displacement Current
points: Ampere's Circuital Law was found to be inconsistent when applied to a charging capacitor.
Maxwell suggested that a changing electric field produces a magnetic field, equivalent to a current.
Displacement current (Id) is that current which comes into existence in a region whenever the electric flux changes with time.
Conduction current (Ic) and displacement current (Id) together maintain continuity.

notes.label: Displacement Current
conditions: Exists in the gap between capacitor plates where E is changing.

notes.label: Ampere-Maxwell Law
conditions: Implies that both conduction current and changing electric field are sources of magnetic field.

notes.title: Maxwell's Equations
points: 1. Gauss's Law for Electricity: Electric flux through closed surface is q/epsilon_0.
2. Gauss's Law for Magnetism: Magnetic flux through closed surface is zero (no monopoles).
3. Faraday's Law: Changing magnetic flux induces electric field.
4. Ampere-Maxwell Law: Magnetic field is produced by conduction current and changing electric flux.

notes.title: Source of Electromagnetic Waves
points: An oscillating charge (accelerating) produces an oscillating electric field, which produces an oscillating magnetic field, and so on.

notes.title: Nature of Electromagnetic Waves
points: EM waves are transverse in nature.
Electric field (E) and Magnetic field (B) oscillate perpendicular to each other and perpendicular to the direction of propagation.
Do not require a material medium for propagation.

notes.title: Propagation of EM Wave
notes.caption: Electric field Ex varies sinusoidally along y-axis, Magnetic field By along z-axis; Wave propagates along x-axis.

notes.label: Speed of EM Waves in Vacuum
conditions: Speed is same for all wavelengths in vacuum.

notes.label: Relation between Field Amplitudes
conditions: E0 and B0 are peak values of electric and magnetic fields.

notes.title: Energy and Momentum
points: EM waves carry energy and momentum.
Energy density is shared equally between electric and magnetic fields.
When waves strike a surface, they exert radiation pressure.

notes.label: Momentum delivered
conditions: U is the total energy transferred to a surface for complete absorption.

notes.title: The Electromagnetic Spectrum
points: Classification of EM waves based on frequency or wavelength.
No sharp division between adjacent regions.
Order (Increasing Frequency): Radio waves, Microwaves, Infrared, Visible, Ultraviolet, X-rays, Gamma rays.

notes.title: Electromagnetic Spectrum
notes.caption: Spectrum showing wavelength ranges from Gamma rays (10^-12 m) to Radio waves (> 10^0 m).

notes.title: Radio Waves
assumptions: Produced by accelerated motion of charges in conducting wires.

result.direction: AM band (530-1710 kHz), FM band (88-108 MHz), TV waves (54-890 MHz).

notes.title: Microwaves
assumptions: Produced by special vacuum tubes (Klystrons, Magnetrons).

result.direction: Used for radar systems in aircraft navigation and cooking food.

notes.title: Infrared Waves
assumptions: Produced by hot bodies and molecules.

result.direction: Maintain earth's warmth; used in physical therapy and night vision.

notes.title: Visible Light
assumptions: Frequency: 4 x 10^14 Hz to 7 x 10^14 Hz.

result.direction: Detectable by human eye. Wavelength 400 nm to 700 nm.

notes.title: Ultraviolet Rays
assumptions: Produced by very hot bodies (Sun) and special lamps.

result.direction: Harmful to skin (tanning, cancer); ozone layer absorbs most solar UV.

notes.title: X-Rays
assumptions: Produced by bombarding metal target with high energy electrons.

result.direction: Used to image bones and treat cancer.

notes.title: Gamma Rays
assumptions: Produced in nuclear reactions and by radioactive nuclei.

result.direction: Highest frequency, highest energy. Used to destroy cancer cells.

ncert_golden_lines: Maxwell famously argued that a changing electric field must induce a magnetic field.
The displacement current has the same physical effects as the conduction current.
Electromagnetic waves transport energy and momentum.
Radio waves are produced by the accelerated motion of charges in conducting wires.
Infrared waves are sometimes referred to as heat waves.

diagram_descriptors.detailed_description: Electric field Ex varies sinusoidally along y-axis, Magnetic field By along z-axis; Wave propagates along x-axis.
Spectrum showing wavelength ranges from Gamma rays (10^-12 m) to Radio waves (> 10^0 m).
